what_it_does: Reports the results of an overnight full-coverage dragnet recall run over session history and Tim's Visual-DNA vault, listing produced extraction assets, the extraction recipe, a crash-and-recovery incident, determine experiments, a no-fiction grounded-determine reliability fix, embedding integration, theorem recall, and follow-up wants.
observations: The run produced 18,857 session-history extractions and 10,568 Visual-DNA extractions stored under .data/store/extractions/.
A stepped adaptive cascade was used: coarse {about,kind,touches} extraction, a step-gate, then fine {summary,entities,claims,relations,open_questions} extraction on gated items.
The session bake crashed at 10,998/18,858 when run_items mis-classified scheme-example lines as addresses; a leading-space guard fixed it and crash-safe resume prevented lost work.
A first determine over the cheap stored extractions recovered design depth, key decisions, and open threads.
A Visual-DNA determine surfaced context-resolution of visual variables and was read as independent confirmation of Tim's meta-pattern.
A narrower self-referential determine confabulated coherent-sounding specifics; the reliability fix was no-fiction claim-grounding.
Grounded determine was built and proven: it clusters real extraction claims by index only, returns verbatim source-cited claims, and runs a no-fiction validity check.
Determine was exposed as an MCP tool via runtime/recall_determine.py.
The keyword candidate filter was found topically blunt; a semantic filter using embeddings of extraction texts fixed it.
29,406 extractions were embedded into the 'extractions' space, unifying dragnet assets with corpus(op='query').
Tim's foundational theorem was baked and embedded, adding 14,592 theorem extractions for a total of 43,991 recall vectors.
Future wants include making determine a tool/skill, wiring RHM/face queries, and reusing the extraction layer for any future question.
standouts: Grounded determine uses index-only clustering so the model cannot invent claim text, structurally preventing confabulation.
Embedding the extraction layer turned the dragnet asset into a cosine-queryable recall surface and fixed the semantic-filter bluntness.
Theorem recall was integrated so the company can query its own foundational base alongside history and design DNA.
The crash-safe/resumable design proved its value during a real mid-run failure.
conventions: Markdown report with section headers, bullet lists, and star-prefixed (★) milestone markers.
Honest gap-surface language and explicit 'default-to-wrong' framing.
Cites commit hashes and file paths as evidence for fixes and tools.
Self-referential evaluation: the file critiques its own determine output.
Mixes quantitative results, design claims, reliability findings, and roadmap items in one document.
concerns: Heavy project-specific jargon makes the file hard to parse without external context.
Self-assessments such as 'proven' and 'trusted' are stated but not independently validated inside the file.
Future work items are embedded within the results section, blurring current status from planned next steps.
No explicit date/author metadata block beyond inline mentions.
notes: A self-evaluating recall-engineering report that documents both the produced extraction assets and the reliability fixes needed to make determine trustworthy.
questions: What is the exact dragnet pipeline and how does it differ from earlier extraction approaches?
What do the coarse and fine extraction schemas look like in code or JSON schema?
Which model and hardware setup is 'local chat-4b' and what are its latency/cost constraints?
How is the 'extractions' embedding space indexed, versioned, and kept in sync with the source chunks?
What is the precise relationship between determine, gap-surface, theorem-mine, and the existing verb set?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to report dragnet recall results; the file also acts as a design notebook, reliability audit, and roadmap for the determine tool, so it does more than a pure results summary.
apparent_themes: Trust through structural no-fiction grounding rather than free synthesis
Context-resolution as a core design law
intention_signals: To show that a cheap, stored extraction layer can support trustworthy, source-traced recall across session history, design DNA, and foundational theorem, and to close the confabulation gap that prevents determine from being a reliable tool.
novelty: It treats the extraction layer itself as an embeddable, queryable recall surface and uses index-only clustering to make synthesis structurally unable to invent claims.
contribution: A concrete, quantified audit trail of building and hardening a recall layer: extraction counts, a real crash/recovery incident, a confabulation finding, a no-fiction grounded-determine fix, embedding integration, and theorem recall — useful as both a record and a pattern for trustworthy corpus recall.
summary_for_embedding: DRAGNET-RESULTS.md is a project report on the overnight dragnet recall run that produced 29,406 extractions from session history and Visual-DNA, fixed a mid-run crash, built a no-fiction grounded determine tool, embedded the extraction layer, and recalled Tim's foundational theorem into a unified queryable corpus.
intention_for_embedding: To record the results, reliability lessons, and next steps from turning raw project history into a trusted, source-traced recall surface before building.
